mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and a decline in consumer price inflation indicates a soft-landing scenario where supply constraints ease without demand destruction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with directional accuracy exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in tightening periods do not significantly underperform baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real consumer credit outstanding reflects expanding household purchasing power and economic momentum, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant positive drift following credit expansion signals.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below the 100 index level signals severe supply chain disruptions or capacity constraints that force input cost inflation and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of threshold breach events, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movement where nonfarm payrolls growth decelerates while housing starts decline indicates synchronized economic cooling across major demand sectors, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain variance in forward returns beyond single-series models, or directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and a rising unemployment rate confirms an economic slowdown that forces equity risk premium expansion, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not significantly decline when both GDP growth decelerates and unemployment rises, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 45% of late-cycle observations.
